mte_summary: Remove ability to add rows in Buyer Work Queue requisitions table (MDR-1071)

test_cases.summary: Regression: Standard Cost Purchase Requisition - 137 PO Conversion

description.scenario: Create a standard cost purchase requisition, approve via BPMS015, convert via BPMS002.1, and verify PO conversion to 137 series.
description.expected_result: Cost purchase requisition approved and converted to 137 series PO successfully

steps.action: Go to Purchase requisition session (tdpur2600m000) and click new.

Maintain header with buyer and business agreement. Create new requisition line with cost item (ZZZCST), quantity, price, confirmed receipt date, business partner, and pegging information (Project and Activity). Save.
Click on Actions and submit PRQ for approval.
Check email inbox for buyer review email. Go to Bizagi and input case number from email.
Open buyer review case. Select RFx required as No, Buyer review completed as Yes. Save and press Next.
steps.expected_result: Buyer Review completed. Case sent for DOA approval

steps.action: Complete DOA approval. Refresh Bizagi case and verify case completed with no outstanding actions.
Go back to Infor LN and refresh purchase requisition screen. Verify PRQ status is Approved.
Go to Bizagi and create a BWQ (BPMS002.1) case. Select buyer, tick purchase requisition tick box, click next.
Select the purchase requisition with correct number. Select transfer against that record, click next.
Refresh the Bizagi case and verify completed with no actions remaining.
Go back to Infor LN and refresh PRQ. Verify status is Converted. Click requisition line > References > Linked Requisition Line Data (tdpur2502s000).
Open purchase order from (tdpur4100m000). Maintain and submit PO for approval.
Complete DOA and supplier response. Open PO in Bizagi BPMS016, complete supplier response ACC.
Refresh PO screen and verify final status is Approved.
Verify no error messages regarding row addition appear in BWQ session.

test_cases.summary: Regression: Subcontracted Purchase Requisition - 137 PO Conversion

description.scenario: Create a subcontracted purchase requisition, approve via BPMS015, convert via BPMS002.1, and verify PO conversion to 137 series.
description.expected_result: Subcontracted purchase requisition approved and converted to 137 series PO successfully

steps.action: Go to Purchase requisition session (tdpur2600m000) and click new.
Create new requisition line with subcontracted item (ZZZSPL-SUBCON-*), quantity, price, confirmed receipt date, business partner, and pegging information (Project and Activity). Save.
Click on Actions and submit PRQ for approval.
Check email inbox for buyer review email. Go to Bizagi and input case number from email.
Open buyer review case. Select RFx required as No, Buyer review completed as Yes. Save and press Next.
steps.expected_result: Buyer Review completed. Case sent for DOA approval

steps.action: Complete DOA approval. Refresh Bizagi case and verify case completed with no outstanding actions.
Go back to Infor LN and refresh purchase requisition screen. Verify PRQ status is Approved.
Go to Bizagi and create a BWQ (BPMS002.1) case. Select buyer, tick purchase requisition tick box, click next.
Select the purchase requisition with correct number. Select transfer against that record, click next.
Refresh the Bizagi case and verify completed with no actions remaining.
Go back to Infor LN and refresh PRQ. Verify status is Converted. Click requisition line > References > Linked Requisition Line Data (tdpur2502s000).
Open purchase order from (tdpur4100m000). Maintain and submit PO for approval.
Complete DOA and supplier response. Open PO in Bizagi BPMS016, complete supplier response ACC.
Refresh PO screen and verify final status is Approved.
Verify no error messages regarding row addition appear in BWQ session.

test_cases.summary: Functional: Buyer Work Queue - Attempt to Add New Row

description.scenario: Attempt to add a new row to the Buyer Work Queue requisitions table to process a requisition not originally in the table.
description.expected_result: System prevents adding new rows. No new row is created in the BWQ requisitions table.

steps.action: Go to Buyer Work Queue session (BPMS002.1) in Bizagi.
Verify a purchase requisition is already present in the table with status Approved.
Locate the button or action intended to add new rows in the BWQ requisitions table.
Click the button to add a new row.
steps.expected_result: System rejects the action or button is disabled

steps.action: Verify that no new row appears in the requisitions table.
Refresh the BWQ screen.
Verify the original requisition remains and no additional rows exist.
Check system logs or error messages for rejection reason.
steps.expected_result: Error message indicating inability to add rows

test_cases.summary: Functional: Buyer Work Queue - Process Existing Approved Requisition

description.scenario: Process an existing approved requisition in the Buyer Work Queue to ensure normal flow is not impacted by the removal of the add row feature.
description.expected_result: Existing approved requisition is successfully processed and transferred.

steps.action: Go to Buyer Work Queue session (BPMS002.1) in Bizagi.
Select an existing purchase requisition with status Approved in the table.
Select the buyer for the requisition.
Click next to initiate the buyer review process.
Complete buyer review (RFx=No, Buyer review=Yes). Save and press Next.
Complete DOA approval if applicable.
Select transfer against the record.
Click next to complete the transfer.
Refresh the BWQ case and verify completed with no actions remaining.
Go back to Infor LN and verify PRQ status is Converted.
